heading: ## Limitations
body:
- **Single-centre design:** Results may not generalise to other healthcare settings or populations.
- **Short follow-up:** Primary endpoint at 6 months; long-term effects (>12 months) are not assessed.
- **Self-reported primary outcome:** PAM-13 is a validated but subjective measure; social desirability bias cannot be excluded.
- **Placebo effect:** No sham intervention for the control arm; differential attention may inflate effect estimates.
- **Sample size assumption:** Standard deviation σ = 10 is based on limited published data; true variance may differ.
- **Technology dependence:** LLM-based intervention may be affected by platform changes, API deprecation, or user digital literacy.
- **Attrition risk:** 20% dropout assumed; higher attrition would reduce statistical power.
- **Generalisability to non-English speakers:** Intervention and instruments are validated in English only.

heading: ## Consortium / partners
body:
- **Lead institution:** [TBD — e.g., University Hospital / Research Institute]

- **Technical partner:** [TBD — e.g., AI/ML lab or health-tech company]

- **Funding body:** [TBD — e.g., National Research Agency / EU Horizon]
- **Regulatory advisor:** [TBD — e.g., Ethics committee liaison / CRO]

heading: ## Risk matrix
body:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|-------------|--------|------------|
| Low recruitment rate | Medium | High | Multi-site recruitment; social media campaign; extended enrolment period |
| High dropout (>20%) | Medium | High | Regular participant engagement; reminder system; financial incentives |
| LLM technical failure (downtime, API errors) | Low | Medium | Redundant server; fallback to rule-based chatbot; real-time monitoring |
| Adverse event rate >5% | Low | High | DSMB review; stopping rule; immediate safety reporting |
| Data privacy breach | Very Low | Very High | End-to-end encryption; pseudonymisation; regular security audits |
| Confounding by digital literacy | Medium | Medium | Stratified randomisation by baseline digital literacy score; sensitivity analysis |
| Seasonal confounding (e.g., flu season) | Low | Low | Balanced enrolment across seasons; covariate adjustment in analysis |
